medications.name: Apixaban
medications.instructions: Take with food. Continue ~3 months minimum from start date (3/2/2027). DO NOT MISS DOSES. AVOID concomitant aspirin, NSAIDs, herbal supplements without checking with prescriber. Hematology follow-up 5/15/2027 to reassess duration.
medications.pharmacy: Hopkins Outpatient Pharmacy / FutureCare Charles Village SNF
medications.notes: Loading dose 10 mg BID 3/2-3/8; maintenance 5 mg BID since 3/9/2027. Patient counseled on bleeding precautions in Vietnamese; written materials provided.

medications.name: Lisinopril
medications.prescriber: Dr. Phong Le (PCP, ongoing); held during AKI 2/15-3/9/2027; resumed at 5 mg
medications.instructions: Take in the AM. PCP to reassess at follow-up 5/2/2027 for possible up-titration once renal function stable.
medications.notes: Dose decreased from 10 to 5 in 1/2026 with CKD progression. Resumed at 5 mg post-admission once Cr near baseline.

medications.name: Atorvastatin
medications.instructions: Take at bedtime. Avoid grapefruit juice. Notify provider for muscle pain.
medications.notes: Continuous since 2015 with brief hold in ICU. LDL at goal.

medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.indication: Chronic joint pain (L knee OA, healed R TKA discomfort); now also post-pancreatitis recovery pain
medications.instructions: Max 3000 mg/day (reduced from 4000 mg/day given mild hepatic insult and chronic alcohol use). Use as needed for pain. Avoid NSAIDs entirely (CKD + anticoagulation).

medications.name: Calcium carbonate
medications.instructions: Take with food. Separate from levothyroxine by ≥4 hours (N/A here).

medications.name: Vitamin D3 (cholecalciferol)
medications.indication: Vitamin D insufficiency (level 28 ng/mL); bone health

medications.name: Multivitamin
medications.indication: General nutritional support; recovery from prolonged hospitalization

medications.name: Potassium chloride
medications.indication: Hypokalemia repletion from prolonged ICU diuresis and tube feeds
medications.instructions: Take with food. Reassess at PCP visit 5/2/2027; goal to discontinue if K stable in normal range.
medications.notes: Temporary post-ICU repletion. Will taper or discontinue at outpatient follow-up.

medications.name: Pantoprazole
medications.indication: Stress ulcer prophylaxis during ICU course; continued through hospitalization
medications.instructions: Take 30 min before breakfast. Will discontinue at 1-month PCP follow-up if no GI symptoms.
medications.notes: Started in MICU; will be evaluated for discontinuation.

reviewedBy: Dr. Anna Reyes (PGY-2) + Pharmacy (discharge med rec) at discharge
_historical_notes: DISCONTINUED 2/15/2027: ibuprofen 400-800 mg PRN (held permanently given CKD progression + new anticoagulation; substitute acetaminophen + topical diclofenac for knee). Aspirin 81 mg daily previously continued for primary prevention despite 2022 USPSTF guidance; discontinued 1/2024 by Dr. Le.
